description: Protected-baseline oracle for the curated photoelectron + auger reference records and the frozen legacy directory (data/xps/elements-main.json, elements-actinides.json, elements-lanthanides.json, auger-lines.json, legacy/). Replaces the brittle whole-file `git diff --quiet HEAD` guards (test_curated_and_legacy_content_unchanged_vs_snapshot in test_machine_tier.py and test_prior_machine_records_unchanged_vs_snapshot in test_conflict_resolution.py), which went spuriously RED on any uncommitted edit and conflated byte-identity with scientific-content identity. 'records' maps transition id -> the whole transition object; 'element_meta' maps '<file>:<symbol>' -> the scientific element fields (symbol, z, name, curation_status, curation_notes) ONLY — additive display/help fields such as short_caveat and the families array are deliberately excluded so they never trip the guard; 'legacy_payloads' maps each legacy file -> its scientific payload. The guards assert every snapshot entry is still present and structurally identical in the live files (mutated value/region/tier/curation_notes/source IS caught); records added later are additive and neither protected nor blocked until this baseline is intentionally extended. THE FIXTURE IS THE SOLE ORACLE — the guards never read git/HEAD. REGENERATION IS A DELIBERATE MANUAL ACT (never inside pytest, never to silence a failure): run `python tests/fixtures/regen_curated_snapshot.py`.

C-1s.notes: Nominal is set to 284.5 — the app's C 1s graphite charge-correction reference — NOT the raw NIST-evaluated value (Powe95, starred: 284.44). C 1s is the calibration anchor, so the reference marker and the CC reference must agree; internal consistency beats the 0.06 eV difference (curator decision, S. Fortier 2026-06). NIST evidence: 20 elemental-form records span 282.0-286.9 incl. the Powe95 evaluated 284.44; NIST compound records (2015 page) extend organic C 1s components to 289.22 — region spans both. Carbonate/fluorocarbon upward extension (>289.5) is a future expansion.

Cl-2p1/2.notes: DERIVED: 198.30 (TPPF90) + 1.60 (DS-2p, BDH74) = 199.90 — no direct NIST 2p1/2 record was archive-recoverable. Needs hand-verification.

Cl-2p3/2.notes: Single NIST elemental-page record (TPPF90, 198.30); splitting from the NIST DS-2p record (BDH74, 1.60). Region sized to span metal chlorides through organochlorine (fallback, unsourced); UCl4-type chlorides sit near the nominal. The narrow 1.6 eV splitting means the doublet often appears as one envelope in surveys. Overlaps U 5p3/2 in actinide chlorides.

Cu-2p1/2.notes: Direct NIST records: 952.56 x2 + 952.55 (Mans94202), 952.45 (MiSi9355) — not derived from the splitting.

Cu-2p3/2.notes: NIST-evaluated value (Seah89, starred); 29 raw records span 932.2-933.1. Splitting = median of NIST DS-2p records (19.8 x3, 19.82 x2, 19.9 x2); note 952.56 - 932.67 = 19.89 disagrees slightly with the DS median — hand-check. Region top covers Cu(II) states (fallback, unsourced); area_ratio 2:1 from 2p degeneracy (2j+1).

Cu-L3M4,5M4,5.notes: NIST notation L3M45M45(1G); KINETIC energy. 25 NIST metal records span 918.1-919.0 with a tight modern cluster at 918.62-918.66 (SSA90 x2 918.62, BiSw80 918.62/.64/.66, AnSe84 918.63/.65, Asam76 918.65); no starred value on the archived page. Region (KE frame) floor extended to cover Cu2O/CuO whose LMM KEs sit lower — fallback, unsourced.

Nb-3d3/2.notes: DERIVED: 202.31 (Powe12) + 2.8 (DS-3d median) = 205.11 — no direct NIST 3d3/2 record was archive-recoverable. Needs hand-verification against live SRD 20.

Nb-3d5/2.notes: NIST-evaluated value (Powe12, 2016 snapshot; the 2004 snapshot's evaluated Powe95 gives 202.34). 14 raw records span 201.5-202.6. Splitting = median of NIST DS-3d records (2.6, 2.75, 2.8 x3); handbooks often cite 2.72 — hand-check. Region top covers Nb(V) oxide (fallback, unsourced); area_ratio 3:2 from 3d degeneracy.

O-1s.notes: Median of 13 NIST records spanning 531.2-533.0 (KBG90, WVBM87, KKMP88, FPT88, TaYa87, KeSc90, RaDe91); no NIST-evaluated (starred) value exists for O 1s. Region floor extended below the NIST span to cover lattice metal oxides — fallback policy, not sourced data.

O-2s.notes: Single NIST record (RaDe91, 28.30 eV) — low-confidence nominal; weak line. Overlaps the U 6p1/2 region in uranium samples.

U-4d3/2.notes: DERIVED: 736.4 + 42.1 (DS-4d median) = 778.5 — no direct NIST elemental 4d3/2 record was archive-recoverable. Needs hand-verification against live SRD 20. Real samples are usually oxidized and appear higher — region spans the NIST-tabulated oxide record U3O7 781.7; top extended to 784.5 for U(VI) (curator-directed margin, 2026-06).

U-4d5/2.notes: Nominal is U METAL: four NIST records 736.0 (FBWF74), 736.4 (ScLa81), 736.7 (AlTu73), 736.8 (ACCT74) — 736.4 chosen as the record nearest the median; hand-check. Real samples are usually oxidized and appear higher — region spans NIST-tabulated oxide records UO2 737.7 / 738.6, U3O7 739.5; top extended to 742.0 for U(VI) (curator-directed margin, 2026-06; UO3 4d values not archive-recoverable). Splitting = median of NIST DS-4d records (42.1 x2, 42.3); area_ratio 3:2 from 4d degeneracy.

U-4f5/2.notes: Nominal is U METAL: direct NIST record (WCSW87); cross-check 377.6 (WCSW87 4f7/2) + 10.8 = 388.4 OK. Real samples are usually oxidized and appear higher — region spans NIST-tabulated oxidized states: D2O/U 388.0-391.5, Cs2U2O7 / Cs2UO4 uranates 391.6; top 393.8 mirrors the 4f7/2 band top (383.0) + DS-4f (10.8) for doublet congruence (curator-directed margin, 2026-06).

U-4f7/2.notes: Nominal is U METAL: median of the 12-record metal cluster (376.5-377.6: HoTh79, VRPC74, AlTu73, ACCT74, Chad73, MCSS82, WRDM79, FBWF74, DMKK84z, ScLa81, WCSW86, WCSW87). Real samples are usually oxidized (UCl4, U4+/U6+) and appear SEVERAL eV HIGHER — the region spans the oxidation states: NIST-tabulated points are the metal cluster, the oxidized-surface record 380.2 (ChGr72), and ~380.8 implied by NIST U(VI) uranate 4f5/2 records (391.6, Cs2U2O7/Cs2UO4) minus DS-4f 10.8; top extended to 383.0 for U(VI) oxides (curator-directed margin, 2026-06). Splitting = median of 9 NIST DS-4f records (10.7-10.9); area_ratio 4:3 from 4f degeneracy.

U-4s.notes: Single NIST record (FBWF74) — low-confidence nominal. Oxidation-widened fallback region (U metal nominal; real samples shift higher).

U-5d3/2.notes: DERIVED: 94.0 + 8.6 (DS-5d, FBWF74) = 102.6 — no direct NIST record archive-recoverable (the 5d3/2 line exists in NIST's v4.1 list). DOUBLE uncertainty: the splitting itself is contested (8.2 vs 8.6). Needs hand-verification.

U-5d5/2.notes: Three NIST records: 93.6 (AlTu73), 94.0 (ScLa81 — median record, chosen), 94.2 (FBWF74). DS-5d records DISAGREE: 8.2 (AlTu73) vs 8.6 (FBWF74) — 8.6 chosen for internal consistency with FBWF74's comprehensive U set; HAND-CHECK. Oxidation-widened fallback region; area_ratio 3:2 from 5d degeneracy.

U-5p3/2.notes: Single NIST record (FBWF74) — low-confidence nominal. Curated UNPAIRED: NIST's line list has no 5p1/2 entry (the DS-5p record, 63.6 eV FBWF74, is noted but unused). Oxidation-widened fallback region. OVERLAPS the Cl 2p region in actinide chlorides (e.g. UCl4) — expect interference.

U-6p1/2.notes: DERIVED: 16.9 + 10.0 (DS-6p, FBWF74) = 26.9 — no direct NIST record archive-recoverable (the 6p1/2 line exists in NIST's v4.1 list). Splitting contested (10.0 vs 10.5). Needs hand-verification. Overlaps the O 2s region (~28.3) in oxidized samples.

U-6p3/2.notes: Three NIST records: 16.8 (FBWF74), 16.9 (WCSW87 — median, chosen), 18.0 (VeLa74). DS-6p records DISAGREE: 10.0 (FBWF74) vs 10.5 (VeLa74) — 10.0 chosen (internally consistent with FBWF74's 16.8); HAND-CHECK. Oxidation-widened fallback region; area_ratio 2:1 from 6p degeneracy.

U-6s.notes: Single NIST record (FBWF74) — low-confidence nominal. Oxidation-widened fallback region.

auger-lines.json:Cu.name: Copper
auger-lines.json:Cu.curation_notes: Curated: L3M4,5M4,5 (1G) — the principal Cu LMM line. Cu2O/CuO LMM kinetic energies (Wagner-plot values) were not recoverable from archived NIST pages — region floor is fallback to cover them; hand-check. NIST also tabulates M3VV (61 eV, PEJ82) and L2-series satellite offsets (SA- records, Scho72 et al.) — not curated in v1. Values recovered from NIST SRD 20 v3.5 via Internet Archive snapshot (2004).

elements-actinides.json:U.name: Uranium
elements-actinides.json:U.curation_notes: Curated: 4f doublet + 4d family. Nominals are U METAL values, but real samples are almost always oxidized (UCl4, U4+/U6+ oxides) and sit several eV HIGHER — expected_region_ev bands are therefore widened upward through the common oxidation states (curator-directed, 2026-06) so orientation bands cover where actual sample peaks appear. U-4d3/2 nominal is DERIVED (4d5/2 + DS-4d median), not a direct NIST record — hand-check. UO2/U3O8/UO3 4f7/2 chemical-state values were NOT archive-recoverable (compound pages 2-5 unarchived) — uncurated; NIST v4.1 line list confirms 4f7/2,sat / 4f5/2,sat satellite records exist but their values were not recoverable — uncurated. Shallow lines (4s, 5d, 5p3/2, 6p, 6s) are curated from the complete 2004 NIST element page. 4p3/2 exists in NIST's v4.1 line list but no value was archive-recoverable — uncurated. U 5f is NOT tabulated as a line in NIST SRD 20 (valence character) — intentionally absent, not an omission. 5p1/2 is not in NIST's line list — 5p3/2 is curated unpaired (the DS-5p record, 63.6 eV FBWF74, is noted but unused). One NIST compound record (U3O7 4d3/2 = 105.3 eV) is a physically impossible data-entry artifact and was discarded. Asymmetric U 4f broadening in U(IV) compounds arises from 5f multiplet coupling — use multiplet models, not single DS peaks (see CLAUDE.md). Values recovered from NIST SRD 20 v3.5/v4.1 via Internet Archive snapshots (2004/2015/2016/2023).

elements-main.json:C.name: Carbon
elements-main.json:C.curation_notes: Curated: 1s. NIST also tabulates a 1s surface core-level shift (SS-1s -0.95, Pate86) — not modeled. C KLL/KVV Auger values were not archive-recoverable — uncurated. Higher-BE chemical states (carbonates ~289.5+, fluorocarbons CF2/CF3) are NOT represented in the recovered NIST records — region top may need upward extension after hand-verification. Values recovered from NIST SRD 20 v3.5/v4.1 via Internet Archive snapshots (2004/2015/2016).

elements-main.json:Cl.name: Chlorine
elements-main.json:Cl.curation_notes: Curated: 2p doublet. The single archive-recoverable NIST elemental-page record (TPPF90) has an unidentified specimen (detail popup unarchived) — its 198.30 eV sits in metal-chloride territory; hand-check against live SRD 20. Chloride-compound 2p values were NOT archive-recoverable (the recovered compound page holds only Cl 1s records near 2815 eV from hard-X-ray work, beyond Al Ka reach) — region is fallback. Cl 2s not recovered — uncurated. In actinide chlorides (e.g. UCl4) the Cl 2p region overlaps U 5p3/2 — expect interference. Values recovered from NIST SRD 20 v4.1 via Internet Archive snapshots (2015/2016).

elements-main.json:Cu.name: Copper
elements-main.json:Cu.curation_notes: Curated: 2p doublet (LMM Auger lives in auger-lines.json). NIST also tabulates 3s (122.2-122.9), 3p3/2 (75.14, Seah89 NIST-evaluated), 2s (1096.4-1097.1), DS-3p (2.2-2.39) — not curated in v1. Cu2O/CuO/Cu(OH)2 chemical-state values and the Cu(II) shake-up satellites (~940-945 eV) were not recoverable from archived NIST pages — uncurated. Values recovered from NIST SRD 20 v3.5/v4.1 via Internet Archive snapshots (2004/2016).

elements-main.json:Nb.name: Niobium
elements-main.json:Nb.curation_notes: Curated: 3d doublet. Nb-3d3/2 nominal is DERIVED (3d5/2 + DS-3d median), not a direct NIST record — the page holding direct 3d3/2 values was not archive-recoverable; hand-check. NIST also tabulates 3p3/2 (360.4-360.7), 3s (466.8-467.1), 4p3/2 (30.77, Powe95 NIST-evaluated), 4s (56.4), DS-3p (15.4-15.5), M5N23V Auger KE (167.6, NIST-evaluated) — not curated in v1. Oxide values (NbO/NbO2/Nb2O5) were not recoverable from archived NIST pages — region tops are fallback. Values recovered from NIST SRD 20 v3.5/v4.1 via Internet Archive snapshots (2004/2016).

elements-main.json:O.name: Oxygen
elements-main.json:O.curation_notes: O KLL Auger KE is NOT curated: NIST tabulates only Auger-parameter records (AP-1s,KVV 1041.5 / 1043.9, KKMP88) whose KE decomposition is ambiguous — needs hand-verification against live SRD 20. NIST 1s records (531.2-533.0) are compound/adsorbate-hosted; the lattice-oxide low end (~529-530.5) is not represented in archive-recoverable records, hence the conservative-fallback region. Values recovered from NIST SRD 20 v3.5/v4.1 via Internet Archive snapshots (2004/2016).

crossrefs.note: KINETIC energy; apparent BE computed at display
crossrefs.marker_note: Auger: marker stays at legacy apparent-BE; corrected KINETIC energy recorded for overlay (apparent BE = hv-KE-wf at display).

crossrefs.note: KINETIC energy; apparent BE computed at display
crossrefs.marker_note: Auger: marker stays at legacy apparent-BE; corrected KINETIC energy recorded for overlay (apparent BE = hv-KE-wf at display).
